nuggets.headline: DAFT PUNK'S "AROUND THE WORLD" WAS REMIXED BY FELLOW FRENCH TOUCH PIONEERS MOTORBASS.
nuggets.text: The repetitive yet mesmerizing beat of "Around the World" captured the essence of the burgeoning French electronic music scene. Another key act in this 'French Touch' movement was the duo Motorbass, made up of Philippe Zdar and Étienne de Crécy. In 1997, they put their own stamp on Daft Punk's hit by releasing the "Motorbass Miami Mix" and "Motorbass Vice Mix" remixes.

source.title: Deeper Than Daft Punk:. Rewinding the disco-driven filter house…
source.quoteSnippet: Daft Punk connection: Motorbass remixed Daft Punk’s “Around The World” in 1997, for the Motorbass Miami Mix and Motorbass Vice mix.

nuggets.headline: DAFT PUNK'S DEBUT "HOMEWORK" WASN'T ORIGINALLY PLANNED AS A COHESIVE ALBUM.
nuggets.text: While you're immersed in "Around the World," consider that its parent album, *Homework*, wasn't initially conceived as a traditionally cohesive record. Daft Punk interspersed their standout singles among more experimental tracks, shaping a dynamic flow that mirrored the raw energy of their early live sets. This creative tension between styles ultimately led to crucial breakthroughs during the recording process.

source.title: Homework: How Daft Punk Schooled Us In The Future Of Dance Music - Dig!
source.quoteSnippet: *Homework* wasn’t truly intended to be an album: the singles are placed between the more experimental tracks in an attempt to form something that felt more traditionally cohesive.

nuggets.imageCaption: Daft Punk pushing boundaries with their debut album.

nuggets.headline: DIVE INTO JUSTICE, A FRENCH DUO LINKED TO DAFT PUNK'S DESIGN CIRCLE.
nuggets.text: If you're digging "Around the World," then you should definitely check out Justice. This Parisian duo is a powerhouse in the same French electronic music scene. The cover art for Alan Braxe's 2005 compilation *The Upper Cuts*—which featured work by Thomas Bangalter—was notably designed by "Death Squad," a collective that included Xavier de Rosnay, one half of Justice. Start with their album *† (Cross)*.

source.title: Alan Braxe
source.quoteSnippet: En 2005, il publie une compilation sur son label Vulture intitulée The Upper Cuts, réunissant les morceaux cités précédemment. Remarquons que la pochette a été réalisée par Death Squad, c'est-à-dire Xavier de Rosnay (moitié du groupe Justice) et Bertrand Lagros de Langeron, connu également sous le nom de So_Me.

nuggets.imageCaption: French electronic duo Justice, connected to Daft Punk's scene.

artistSummary: Daft Punk, the pioneering French electronic music duo of Thomas Bangalter and Guy-Manuel de Homem-Christo, transformed dance music into a global cultural force. They were known for their innovative blend of house rhythms with pop sensibilities and their iconic robot personas.
